This stage is cross-cutting runtime infrastructure: it sits underneath startup checks, interactive command handling, git-backed workspace inspection, plugin install/upgrade flows, and process execution on every platform. Its shell layer starts with shell-command, which detects concrete shells, parses command text, and applies safety rules; core/src/shell.rs then turns those results into the normalized shell descriptors and argv forms used system-wide, while protocol/src/shell_environment.rs builds the exact inherited, filtered, and overridden environment passed to child shells. tui/src/exec_command.rs and tui/src/external_editor.rs adapt that machinery for user-facing command display, parsing, and editor launching.

Execution support is rounded out by core/src/tools/mod.rs, unified_exec/head_tail_buffer.rs, utils/pty/src/process.rs, linux-sandbox/src/exec_util.rs, windows_common.rs, cli/src/exit_status.rs, and the macOS pid tracker, which together prepare argv/env, manage spawned sessions, bound captured output, preserve platform-specific process semantics, and support sandbox diagnostics.

Git utilities provide safe, side-effect-free repository access: git-utils exposes operations, fsmonitor policy, branch resolution, and repository metadata/diff collection, which tui/src/get_git_diff.rs uses for visible workspace diffs. Plugin packaging helpers archive, clone, stage, activate, and roll back marketplace content safely. Smaller utilities normalize executable names and support fuzzy patch-context matching.
